article.title: Local SEO for Small Business - Get Found in Google Maps and Local Search
article.body_markdown:
46% of all Google searches have local intent. If you run a local business - restaurant, plumber, dentist, salon - local SEO is everything.

## 1. Claim Your Google Business Profile

This is step one. Verify your business. Add photos, hours, services, and respond to reviews. A complete profile ranks higher.

## 2. Get Local Citations

List on Yelp, Yellow Pages, industry directories, and chamber of commerce sites. Consistent Name, Address, Phone across all listings is critical.

## 3. Write Location-Specific Content

Do not just blog about your industry. Blog about your industry IN your location. "How to choose a dentist in Austin" ranks better than "how to choose a dentist."

## 4. Collect Reviews

Reviews are a confirmed local ranking factor. Ask happy customers. Respond to every review.

I built [SEO Spark](https://seospark.net) to help local businesses create location-specific content in 60 seconds. Free tier: 3 articles.
